---
created: "2026-03-22T00:00:00Z"
id: task-sv0vm-bytecode-format
key: sv0vm-bytecode-format
state: draft
tags:
    - sv0
    - sv0vm
    - bytecode
title: "sv0vm bytecode format definition"
type: task
---

# sv0vm bytecode format definition

## purpose

design and implement the sv0 bytecode format. the bytecode format is the
binary representation of sv0-IR that sv0vm executes. it must be compact,
fast to decode, and expressive enough to represent all sv0-IR operations.

the bytecode format should be documented in sv0doc so both sv0c (encoder)
and sv0vm (decoder) implement the same specification.

## dependencies

/require: sml

## configuration

/env: SV0VM_ROOT=sv0vm
/env: SV0DOC_ROOT=sv0doc
/env: SRC_DIR=sv0vm/src

## implementation

### step 1: instruction set design

/ai: design the sv0 bytecode instruction set.

     the VM is a stack-based machine. design opcodes for:

     stack operations:
     - PUSH_I32 <value>    push 32-bit integer
     - PUSH_I64 <value>    push 64-bit integer
     - PUSH_F64 <value>    push 64-bit float
     - PUSH_BOOL <value>   push boolean
     - PUSH_STRING <index> push string from constant pool
     - PUSH_UNIT           push unit value ()
     - POP                 discard top of stack
     - DUP                 duplicate top of stack

     arithmetic (pop operands, push result):
     - ADD_I32, SUB_I32, MUL_I32, DIV_I32, MOD_I32
     - ADD_I64, SUB_I64, MUL_I64, DIV_I64, MOD_I64
     - ADD_F64, SUB_F64, MUL_F64, DIV_F64
     - NEG_I32, NEG_I64, NEG_F64

     comparison:
     - EQ, NEQ, LT, GT, LTE, GTE
     - (operate on top two stack values, push bool)

     logic/bitwise:
     - AND, OR, NOT
     - BIT_AND, BIT_OR, BIT_XOR, BIT_NOT
     - SHL, SHR

     local variables:
     - LOAD_LOCAL <slot>   push value from local variable slot
     - STORE_LOCAL <slot>  pop and store into local variable slot

     control flow:
     - JUMP <offset>       unconditional jump
     - JUMP_IF <offset>    jump if top of stack is true
     - JUMP_IF_NOT <offset> jump if top of stack is false
     - CALL <func_index> <arg_count>  call function
     - RETURN              return from function
     - CALL_BUILTIN <id>   call built-in function

     memory:
     - ALLOC_STRUCT <type_index>  allocate struct on heap
     - GET_FIELD <field_index>    get struct field
     - SET_FIELD <field_index>    set struct field
     - ALLOC_ARRAY <count>        allocate array
     - GET_INDEX                  array index access (bounds-checked)
     - SET_INDEX                  array index store

     contracts:
     - CONTRACT_CHECK <msg_index>  pop bool, abort with message if false

     enum/tagged union:
     - CONSTRUCT_VARIANT <type_index> <variant_index> <field_count>
     - GET_TAG                      get variant tag
     - GET_VARIANT_FIELD <index>    get variant field

     casting:
     - CAST <from_type> <to_type>  type conversion

     document the instruction set in sv0doc/bytecode/instructions.md.

### step 2: binary format

/ai: design the binary file format (.sv0b) for sv0 bytecode.

     the file should contain:
     - magic bytes: "SV0B"
     - version: u16
     - constant pool: strings, type descriptors
     - function table: name, arity, local count, bytecode offset
     - bytecode section: raw instruction bytes
     - debug info section (optional): source spans for each instruction

     implement the encoder (for sv0c's VM backend) and decoder (for sv0vm)
     as SML modules.

/run: 01-define-bytecode.sh

### step 3: bytecode tests

/ai: write tests for bytecode encoding/decoding:
     - round-trip: encode instructions -> decode -> verify match
     - file format: write .sv0b file -> read back -> verify
     - all instruction types covered

/run: 02-test-encoding.sh

## validation

/assert: test -f $SRC_DIR/bytecode/bytecode.sml
